published_prior_art.cite: Read2022/Guay2021 give sign-zero algorithms and higher-moment checks, but not the three-diagnostic tuple

repo_prior_art.cite: parent eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1 expected under upgrade mode; no non-parent duplicate found

published_prior_art.cite: DrautzburgWright2023 and CarrieroMarcellinoTornese2024 blend/refine sign restrictions with statistical identification, but do not give this LP/non-Gaussian support-gap mechanism bound

repo_prior_art.cite: parent eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1 expected under upgrade mode; no non-parent duplicate found

published_prior_art.cite: PlagborgMollerWolf2021 gives common-restriction equivalence, not an iff frontier for omitted non-Gaussian shock-law restrictions

novelty_flags.label: upgrade_review
novelty_flags.one_line: PN5 cluster lock is only partially met: the parent proposal locks a partial-identification/support-function anchor, while this draft relocks as exact identification even though §8 still studies support gaps.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Mechanism-axis delta is partial: the sign and cumulant channels are named, but the pasting channel is defined as P(P)=Q_SVAR, so it is not isolated from the cumulant/SVAR constraint.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Misses Carriero, Marcellino, and Tornese 2024, 'Blended identification in structural VARs,' a close comparator combining sign restrictions with statistical shock identification and discussing masquerading.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship claim cites PlagborgMollerWolf2021, Wolf2022, LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017, and DrautzburgWright2023 without section/equation/page anchors to the specific gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The iff frontier is anchored only by bibkeys, with no location-level pointer in PlagborgMollerWolf2021 or the non-Gaussian SVAR papers.

novelty_flags.label: novelty_target
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until the pasting mechanism is made mathematically distinct and the comparator chain is location-anchored.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Common-law pasting is not a well-posed separate object: LP feasibility already uses one rotation Q for all horizons, and P(P) is then set equal to Q_SVAR.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2 / §9 parent reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Under parent collapse τ becomes c_w(P) m_delta4, so τ=χ_w(P) does not follow unless χ_w(P)=c_w(P)m_delta4 is explicitly imposed or definitions are changed.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / notation
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked block names L_w^{cum} and P_w^{paste}, while §6-§8 use Lambda_w^{cum} and Pi_w^{paste}; the cluster label also drifts from the parent support-function partial-ID anchor.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft names mechanism diagnostics, but one channel collapses into Q_SVAR and the parent-cluster reduction is not algebraically pinned.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite the locked tuple to match the parent support-function/partial-ID cluster, or explicitly justify a true exact-ID upgrade with point-identification statements rather than support gaps.
Redefine the LP relaxation so horizonwise LP rows may be chosen separately, then define a genuine pasting defect measuring failure to share one cumulant-diagonalizing law.
Patch the parent-collapse algebra by defining the collapsed cumulant channel as χ_w(P)/c_w(P), or add an explicit assumption equating c_w(P)m_delta4 with the parent margin.
Add location-level anchors for PMW 2021, Wolf 2022, Drautzburg-Wright 2023, and Carriero-Marcellino-Tornese 2024, then state exactly which section-level claim each §8 result tightens.

recommended_next_step: Revise the mechanism formalization before Stage 0: fix the cluster lock, make pasting non-redundant, and repair the parent-reduction identity.
